data.title: RayJay tweaks ratings on U.S. Bancorp, other banks ahead of 2025
data.description:
Raymond James collectively tweaked its rating recommendations on 11 banks within its coverage universe heading into the new year.

With President-elect Donald Trump set to take office in January, analyst Michael Rose favors banks that stand to gain the most from a deregulatory environment, as well as positive operating leverage and a steeper yield curve, he wrote in a note to clients.

He also noted that he prefers community and/or small-mid cap banks "that should benefit both fundamentally from an improving operating backdrop and use their premium currencies to deploy excess capital into smaller acquisitions where there are a relatively large number of opportunities to do so."

The rating changes include: Business First (NASDAQ:BFST), upgraded to Outperform from Market Perform; Commerce Bancshares (NASDAQ:CBSH), downgraded to Market Perform from Outperform, Hilltop Holdings (NYSE:HTH), downgraded to Underperform from Market Perform, Hancock Whitney (NASDAQ:HWC), upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform, Origin Bancorp (NYSE:OBK), upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform, OceanFirst (OCFCO, upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform, Old Second (OSBC), upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform, First Financial (NASDAQ:THFF), upgraded to Outperform from Market Perform, United Community (NYSE:UCB), upgraded to Outperform from Market Perform, and USCB Financial (NASDAQ:USCB), downgraded to Outperform from Strong Buy.

RayJay also upped U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) to Outperform from Market Perform, owing to its prospects for producing positive operating leverage in 2025 and 2026.

Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha's Quant system gives Northeast Community Bancorp (NECB) the highest rating among regional bank stocks, followed by Third Coast Bancshares (TCBX) and M&T Bank (MTB).

data.title: Arm, Qualcomm set to make closing arguments in legal spat: report
data.description:
Lawyers representing Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) and Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) could make closing arguments in the legal dispute between the two tech companies on Friday, Reuters reported.

Shares of both Arm and Qualcomm fell nearly 2% in late morning trading on Thursday.

The spat stems from whether Qualcomm-owned Nuvia breached a licensing agreement with Arm. While Nuvia and Qualcomm are licensors of Arm's technology, Qualcomm believed its acquisition of Nuvia would allow it to pay lower royalties than it had previously, something Arm objected to.

If Arm wins the case — which started on Monday — it could result in Qualcomm having to end the Nuvia technology, which was partially the basis for its chips that are now being used by Microsoft (MSFT) for some of its PCs. Qualcomm may also have to pay damages to Arm, depending upon the jury's findings.

The trial is being heard by an eight-person jury in a Delaware federal court.

San Diego-based Qualcomm purchased Nuvia in March 2021 for $1.4B and said at the time it expected to integrate Nuvia's products "across a wide portfolio of products," including smartphones, laptops, advanced driver assistance systems and infrastructure networking solutions.

Following that, there were rumors that Qualcomm could make chips for the server market thanks to the Nuvia acquisition, but those plans have not come to light so far.

A verdict could come in the next few days, the news outlet added.

data.title: Aquestive receives FDA Orphan Drug Exclusivity for Libervant
data.description:
Aquestive Therapeutics (NASDAQ:AQST) has received FDA Orphan Drug Exclusivity for its drug Libervant Buccal Film in the treatment of seizure clusters in children aged two to five years, extending the company’s market exclusivity to April 2031.

The seven-year market exclusivity was granted for the acute treatment of intermittent, stereotypic episodes of frequent seizure activity that are distinct from a patient’s usual seizure pattern in patients with epilepsy between two and five years of age, according to a statement.

Libervant Buccal Film, a formulation of the drug diazepam, was approved for the treatment of children aged two to five years in April 2024. The product was originally granted Orphan Drug Designation in November 2016.

The company noted that Libervant Buccal Film is the only FDA-approved orally administered rescue product for the treatment of seizure clusters in patients with epilepsy between two and five years of age.

data.title: Banzai completes acquisition of OpenReel
data.description:
• Banzai International (NASDAQ:BNZI) has successfully closed its previously announced acquisition of OpenReel, a leading digital video creation platform.
  • Additionally, the company announced that it plans to exceed the top end of its previously disclosed revenue guidance of $10M in FY 2024.
  • Banzai issued an aggregate of 0.93M shares of Class A Common Stock, and 11.77M Pre-Funded Warrants exercisable for shares of Class A Common Stock in lieu thereof, equal to $19.6M.
  • OpenReel’s enterprise customer base includes global organizations, such as Bristol Myers Squibb, Ingram Micro, DXC Technology, Insider and US Steel.
  • Acquiring mission-critical MarTech products like OpenReel will allow Banzai to drive customer growth by offering new solutions to existing customers to create long-term value for our shareholders.

data.title: Trump and Warren agree on one thing - end the debt ceiling
data.description:
President-elect Donald Trump and Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) disagree on most things, except one — the U.S. should eliminate its debt ceiling.

The debt ceiling is set by lawmakers to determine how much the federal government can borrow to pay its bills. But it doesn't authorize new spending. The only other country that has a debt ceiling is Denmark.

In an interview with NBC News, Trump said that Congress should get rid of the limit, suggesting that it's a meaningless concept. "It doesn't mean anything, except psychologically," he told NBC.

"The Democrats have said they want to get rid of it. If they want to get rid of it, I would lead the charge," he said.

After the news of Trump's position on the debt limit, Warren wrote on X, "I agree with President-elect Trump that Congress should terminate the debt limit and never again govern by hostage taking."

For years, Congress has voted to raise the debt limit to allow the Treasury to raise the funds for legislation that has already been passed, but it's often framed as a battle over spending amid shows of political brinkmanship. In June 2023, Congress suspended the debt ceiling until Jan. 1, 2025, after the U.S. Treasury used extraordinary measure to fund the government once the debt ceiling was exceeded.

Trump made the comments the day after he came out against the bipartisan stopgap spending deal that had been reached earlier in the week. The topic of the debt ceiling had not even been broached in crafting the continuing resolution for spending that would have extended funding to March 14.

But the bipartisan stopgap agreement appears to be doomed, almost assuring a Christmas-time government shutdown, once Trump opposed it, saying it included too many "Democrat giveaways."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: High spirits: Diageo mulls over a sale of the Cîroc Vodka brand
data.description:
Diageo Plc (NYSE:DEO) is exploring a sale of Cîroc Vodka, according to Bloomberg. Sources indicate that the company has reached out to potential suitors, including beverage companies and buyout firms, to check on potential interest.

Cîroc is a premium vodka brand established in 2003 by Jean-Sébastien Robicquet in France. Distinctively, it is made from fine French grapes rather than the traditional grains used in most vodkas. The unique base is seen as setting it apart in the competitive spirits market.

Diageo (DEO) is considering the sale of Cîroc following the settlement in January of a lawsuit with Sean Combs, in which he accused the spirits company of racism in its alleged neglect of Cîroc Vodka and the DeLeón Tequila brand. The music mogul was recently arrested by federal agents in New York on charges of racketeering and sex trafficking.

The high-end spirits category as a whole has slumped in 2024, due in part to softer demand in China. Sales of Cîroc fell 28% in North America for the fiscal year ending end of June, according to Diageo's (DEO) annual report.

Diageo (DEO) traded 0.7% higher in Thursday morning trading. The spirits stock is still down 13% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: 25 U.S. ‘highest conviction’ stocks – UBS
data.description:
UBS analysts updated their “highest conviction calls,” which are 25 of their top stocks for 2025.

These are the 25 “most compelling buy-rated investment ideas for the year ahead,” said Joseph Parkhill, analyst at UBS. “We've focused on stocks where we believe our analysts have a differentiated view vs. consensus, and where we have interesting or proprietary data sources.”

Analysts also consider the potential upside to each stock’s price target, the risk/reward skew, and each of the stock's exposure to important themes for the next year.

data.title: Gilead raises Assembly Bio stake to ~30%
data.description:
Shares of Assembly Biosciences (NASDAQ:ASMB) traded higher on Thursday after Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) agreed to increase its ownership in the antiviral developer to 29.9% following an equity investment of $20.1M.

Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD), a leader in the antiviral market, previously held a 19.9% stake in Assembly Bio (NASDAQ:ASMB), and following a 2023 deal, the duo began to collaborate against viral infections, including those resulting from the hepatitis D virus (HDV).

Concurrently, the company announced an amendment to its collaboration agreement with Gilead (GILD) to indicate an accelerated timeline to receive $10M in option-related payments for its development work on ABI-6250, an oral antiviral targeting HDV.

“Gilead’s further investment strengthens our balance sheet as we look ahead to multiple key clinical data readouts for our novel antiviral candidates in 2025,” Assembly Bio CEO Jason Okazaki said.

With the accelerated payments and new equity investment from GILD, the company projects its cash runway to extend into mid-2026.

data.title: BlackRock said to write down renewable fund, enlist GIP execs for turnaround
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) is slashing the value of one of its flagship renewable funds following the failure of two key investments, while enlisting leadership from its Global Infrastructure Partners arm to steer the fund's recovery, according to a media report dated Thursday.

The markdowns were triggered by failed investments in now-defunct renewable firms Northvolt and SolarZero, Bloomberg reported, citing a letter to clients written by GIP executives. That, in turn, prompted a wider review of the portfolio's outlook.

A review of assets in BlackRock's (BLK) Global Renewable Power Fund III dropped its net internal rate of return to -0.3% by the end of Q3, down from 11.5% in Q2, the article said. In 2021, the fund had a final close of $4.8B.

“These revisions are driven by adjusted market assumptions and updated business plans from individual portfolio companies,” GIP President Raj Rao and GIP partner Bruce MacLennan wrote in the letter, which Bloomberg had reviewed.

“We have also revised certain assumptions GRP was using for discount rates and potential exit valuations for renewables development assets to align with the current market outlook,” they added.

In an effort to improve the portfolio's value, MacLennan will take charge of the funds, collaborating with David Giordano, who initiated the investments, along with other GIP and BlackRock (BLK) staff.

Last month, BlackRock (BLK) added GIP chief Bayo Ogunlesi to its board.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow rise a day after Fed-fueled selloff
data.description:
The major averages pushed higher on Thursday, a day after Wall Street tumbled as the Federal Reserve projected fewer rate cuts in 2025.

Early on and the benchmark S&P 500 futures (SP500) was +0.2%, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) moved to +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.56%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 5 basis points to 4.30%.

U.S. stocks on Wednesday slumped after the Federal Reserve projected higher inflation and fewer rate cuts in 2025. The estimates came after the central bank announced a quarter-point rate cut, as widely expected.

"There might only be six days until Christmas, but markets still had time for another surprise yesterday, as a hawkish cut from the Fed saw the S&P 500 post its biggest decline after a Fed meeting since 2001," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

While the Fed delivered another 25 basis point rate cut on Wednesday, the overall messaging from the central bank was a much slower pace of policy easing in 2025.

"The moves led to a significant cross-asset slump, with the 10 year Treasury yield closing above 4.5% for the first time since May, whilst the VIX index of volatility surged, and touched its highest since the market turmoil back in the summer," Allen added.

The Fed cut rates as expected, but hawkish forecasts and press conference language surprised markets, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

As for Thursday's economic calendar, the U.S. GDP was revised up to 3.1% in its third estimate, while initial jobless claims fell more than expected in past week.

Moreover, the U.S. existing home sales rose 4.8% M/M in November to 4.15M, topping the 4.09M consensus level. Also, the U.S. Leading Indicator Index surprised economists to the upside in November.

The Fed Balance Sheet will also be released later in the day.
